mission.title: kagenti: bug/question: Keycloak connection recovery (long-lived KIND cluster?)
mission.description: bug/question: Keycloak connection recovery (long-lived KIND cluster?). This issue affects 0+ users.

steps.title: Identify kagenti troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kagenti deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kagenti -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kagenti
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kagenti -n kagenti --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kagenti configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kagenti configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kagenti -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kagenti
kubectl get configmap -n kagenti -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kagenti
```
### Describe the bug

I’ve left a kagenti installation for a couple days each time but it will puzzlingly stop working. The containers all stay up, but it is unclear to me how to ‘reset’ the connections where necessary.

steps.title: Apply the fix for bug/question: Keycloak connection recovery (long-lived KIND
steps.description:
## Summary

By making Postgres stateful, we avoid data loss in case of Postgres pod restarts. This fix is also trying to address the issue #115 - but need longer term testing to assess if it helps (restart of both Postgres and keycloak may still be required

Fixes: should address #115 but to be
```yaml
2025-08-18 16:07:51,552 WARN  [io.agroal.pool] (agroal-11) Datasource '<default>': The connection attempt failed.
2025-08-18 16:07:51,553 WARN  [org.hibernate.engine.jdbc.env.internal.JdbcEnvironmentInitiator] (JPA Startup Thread) HHH000342: Could not obtain connection to query metadata: org.hibernate.exception.JDBCConnectionException: unable to obtain isolated JDBC connection [The connection attempt failed.] [n/a]
```

steps.title: Confirm bug/question: Keycloak connection recovery is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kagenti -n kagenti --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kagenti --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
## Summary

By making Postgres stateful, we avoid data loss in case of Postgres pod restarts. This fix is also trying to address the issue #115 - but need longer term testing to assess if it helps (restart of both Postgres and keycloak may still be required

Fixes: should address #115 but to be verified
codeSnippets:
2025-08-18 16:07:51,552 WARN  [io.agroal.pool] (agroal-11) Datasource '<default>': The connection attempt failed.
2025-08-18 16:07:51,553 WARN  [org.hibernate.engine.jdbc.env.internal.JdbcEnvironmentInitiator] (JPA Startup Thread) HHH000342: Could not obtain connection to query metadata: org.hibernate.exception.JDBCConnectionException: unable to obtain isolated JDBC connection [The connection attempt failed.] [n/a]

kubectl rollout restart statefulset -n keycloak postgres 
sleep 10
kubectl rollout restart statefulset -n keycloak keycloak 
# cd to Kagenti installer dir
# need to re-run installer as the client config got lost even if we had the PVC for Postgres
uv run kagenti-installer. 
# if an agent was not working then need also to restart agent - e.g.
 kubectl rollout restart -n team1 deployment weather-service

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kagenti installed or the issue environment reproducible.
